summary: First-iteration full backend + frontend testing of EcoMind. Backend 27/27 pytest tests passed (auth, dashboard, chat streaming, prompt copilot, analytics, gamification, settings, PDF report, admin). Frontend authenticated flows all working (register→dashboard, all 9 stat cards, sidebar 9/9 nav items, Copilot analyze+optimize+accept with confetti, Chat SSE streaming with Claude reply 'Hi there, friend!', analytics 4 recharts + 7/14/30d buttons, leaderboard, achievements, reports, settings, profile, login). CRITICAL BUG: Landing page (/) crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading ReactCurrentOwner)' — @react-three/fiber@8.15.19 is incompatible with React 19.0.0.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught runtime error 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading ReactCurrentOwner)'. Entire landing page (/) fails to render — user sees only a red 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay. Blocks the primary entry point of the app. Users can only reach the app by manually navigating to /register or /login.

action_items: CRITICAL: Upgrade @react-three/fiber from 8.15.19 → ^9.5.0 (React 19-compatible) AND @react-three/drei from 9.99.0 → ^10.x (or v9.116+). Command: `cd /app/frontend && yarn add @react-three/fiber@^9 @react-three/drei@latest` then restart frontend. This restores the 3D globe landing page. Verified via web_search: v9 bundles a React 19 reconciler that fixes ReactCurrentOwner error.
Optional: Wrap Globe in an ErrorBoundary so a future r3f incompatibility doesn't take down the whole landing page.
critical_code_review_comments: Dependency mismatch: package.json pins React 19.0.0 with @react-three/fiber 8.15.19 — v8 only supports React 18. This is the root cause of the landing-page crash.
auth.py get_current_user: bearer token first tries JWT then falls back to session_token — good pattern. Session cookie set with SameSite=None + Secure = correct for cross-site OAuth.
routes.py: All Mongo reads project `{"_id": 0}` — no ObjectId serialization issue observed. Good.
chat_stream fetches `sess` but references it later inside async generator; still works because it's captured in closure and title auto-update logic looks at old title correctly.

gamification challenges query uses `{'created_at': {'$regex': '^today'}}` — relies on created_at being stored as ISO string (which serialize() does). Works but string-regex on dates is fragile; consider indexed range query on ISO strings.
routes.py is 560 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into chat.py / prompts.py / admin.py modules for maintainability.
ai_service.py suggest_completion_llm can return long strings; already truncated to 12 words — good.
Frontend Chat.jsx uses regenerate() which sets input then setTimeout(send, 50) — mildly hacky but functional.

success_rate.frontend: ~92% (all authenticated pages work; only landing page / crashes)

test_credentials: Backend test user auto-generated per run (test_<uuid>@ecomind.app / test1234). UI test created uitest_<epoch>@ecomind.app / test1234 successfully.
seed_data_creation: None manually seeded — all data created dynamically via /api/auth/register + /api/prompt/optimize + /api/chats/stream during tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is rock-solid (27/27). Focus retest on: (1) After landing page fix, verify 3D globe loads at /, verify hero CTAs (getstarted/demo/learnmore) navigate correctly, stats+features scroll. (2) Google Sign-in button click should redirect to auth.emergentagent.com — could not verify because landing crashed before we reached login (login-page Google button verified visible/clickable but did not click through). (3) Admin panel UI: promoted a test user to admin in mongo during backend test — main agent should verify the /admin route renders users table + role toggle in the UI. (4) Reports page 'Download PDF' — backend PDF endpoint verified (200 + %PDF magic), but frontend button click was not exercised in UI test.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: React 19.0.0 removed internal API `ReactCurrentOwner` from `react-dom`. @react-three/fiber v8 relies on this legacy internal via its custom reconciler, causing the crash `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'ReactCurrentOwner')` during Globe render. Reproduction: navigate to https://carbon-sync-test.preview.emergentagent.com/ — user sees only red error overlay. Mitigation: upgrade @react-three/fiber to v9 (stable v9.5+ bundles a React 19-compatible reconciler) and @react-three/drei to a compatible version (v10.x or v9.116+). Confirmed via web_search — pmndrs officially states v9 is required for React 19.
